note: Loose upright minuscule with pronounced ligatures

note.value: Decorative bars in red and blue
Previous catalogs dated this manuscript to the 13th-14th c. CE

note: Minuscule "à μεν distendu" | Variant of the epsilon style characterized by thick vertical strokes, triangular α, θ in the shape of biblical majuscule, and featuring the ligature "distendu" of μεν and γεν. Initials and titles are written in epigraphische Auszeichnungsmajuskel
The lower writing runs parallel to the overtext
Each side preserves 22–23 lines of text arranged in a single column and spaced 6 mm apart

One folio of Sin. gr. 960 corresponds to a single folio of the original manuscript, the likely size of the original being 180x140 mm (though doubtless with some trimming of the height). The Schriftspiegel is approximately 125x95 mm. The outer margin measures 30 mm, the lower 24 mm, and the upper 14 mm

text_unit.label: Liturgy of Basil the Great and of the Presanctified Gifts

rel_con.label: Orthodox Eastern Church. Liturgy of St. Basil

work_wit.alt_title: Liturgy of Basil the Great and of the Presanctified Gifts

note.value: The preserved sections from the Liturgy of Saint Basil come from the anaphora and the dismissal rite. A notable gap occurs between fol. 142v and fol. 145r: among other items, the epiclesis and intercessions for the living and the dead (diptychs) are missing. The beginning of the Liturgy of the Presanctified Gifts is found on fol. 146v, line 19

Bibliography: G. Rossetto, Greek Palimpsests at Saint Catherine's Monastery (Sinai): Three Euchologia as Case Studies, Vienna 2022 ; Arranz = M. Arranz, L’eucologio costantinopolitano agli inizi del secolo XI. Hagiasmatarion & Archieratikon (Rituale & Pontificale) con l’aggiunta del Leiturgikon (Messale). Rome 1996 ; Goar = J. Goar, Εὐχολόγιον sive Rituale Graecorum complectens ritus et ordines Divinae Liturgiae, officiorum, sacramentorum, consecrationum, benedictionum, funerum, orationum editio secunda expurgata et accuratior. Venice (Graz) 1730 (19602)
Description of undertext by Giulia Rossetto

Bibliography: G. Rossetto, Greek Palimpsests at Saint Catherine's Monastery (Sinai): Three Euchologia as Case Studies, Vienna 2022 ; Arranz = M. Arranz, L’eucologio costantinopolitano agli inizi del secolo XI. Hagiasmatarion & Archieratikon (Rituale & Pontificale) con l’aggiunta del Leiturgikon (Messale). Rome 1996 ; Goar = J. Goar, Εὐχολόγιον sive Rituale Graecorum complectens ritus et ordines Divinae Liturgiae, officiorum, sacramentorum, consecrationum, benedictionum, funerum, orationum editio secunda expurgata et accuratior. Venice (Graz) 1730 (19602)

note: Small and elegant upright minuscule, conforming with the “Perlschrift” style. The writing runs across the ruled lines. The first letter of each sticheron is written in epigraphic majuscule and located outside the Schriftspiegel. Initials and rubrics are written in red. The margins are sometimes used by the scribe to add musical and liturgical information, such as the indication of echos
The musical notation is neumatic and corresponds to a stage between Coislin IV and Coislin V (see Floros 1970), which suggests that the undertext was copied in the second half of the 11th c.
The undertext runs parallel to the overtext
Each side preserves 19–20 lines of text (the distance between the lines is 6 mm), which are arranged in one column

A single folio of Sin. gr. 960 corresponds to a single folio of the original manuscript, and thus the original manuscript was probably of similar dimensions, approximately 160x150 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 130x85 mm. The outer margin measures 35 mm and the lower, 30 mm (f. 21r)

Prickings are still visible in the margins where there is little trimming (e.g. fol. 13 and 109), their shape elongated rather than round and probably made with a penknife. Ruled lines correspond to Sautel-Leroy type 22C1b

note.value: The poor condition of the undertext does not allow a complete folio by folio text identification; however, the content of the identified folia can be summarized as follows: 1) Stichera for the fixed year; 2) Stichera for the movable paschal cycle; 3) Octotonal hymnody of the Sunday Vigil service

Bibliography: G. Rossetto, Greek Palimpsests at Saint Catherine's Monastery (Sinai): Three Euchologia as Case Studies, Vienna 2022 ; SAV = Chr. Troelsgård, A List of Sticheron Call-Numbers of the Standard Abridged Version of the Sticherarion. Part I (The Cycle of the Twelve Months). Cahiers de l’Institut du Moyen-Âge Grec et Latin 74 (2003) 3–20 ; Vind. Theol. gr. 136 = G. Wolfram, Sticherarium antiquum Vindobonense: Codex theol. gr. 136 Bibliothecae Nationalis Austriacae phototypice depictus. Pars principalis & Pars suppletoria. Vienna 1987 ; Floros 1970, Universale Neumenkunde. I–III. Kassel 1970

Since the overtext runs so precisely over the undertext, and since the latter has been well erased, the lower layer is particularly hard to recover. The text on fols. 10, 15, 16, 19, 22, 26, 29, 39, 49, 78, 79, 81, 82, 84, 89, 149, 150, 151, 153, 154 has so far been identified
This same undertext is also found in Sinai Greek NF M 11 and Sinai Greek NF M 22
